glossary: A1 ≈ EN1 (aerobic base) · A2 ≈ EN2 · A3 / VO2 ≈ EN3 / SP1 · RP ≈ SP2 (race pace) · SP ≈ SP3. Older cruise-time and R/V zone names vary by coach — use the system your team uses. Fixed and percentage offset models both anchor to goal race average; treat paces as ±1–3 s per 100. HR ranges are general estimates; confirm targets with your coach.

fixed.label: Fixed seconds
fixed.description: Same +/− seconds per 100 regardless of speed (simple cruise-style ladder).

percent.label: Percentage
percent.description: Scales with your pace (Hy-Tek-style); better for very fast or slow swimmers.

a-system.label: A-system (REC, A1, A2, A3, VO2, RP, SP)

dual.label: Dual labels (A + US codes)
dual.description: Both naming systems side by side

recovery.reliabilityNote: Coaches usually set recovery paces from feel or current threshold, not goal race time.

lowAerobic.reliabilityNote: Coaches usually set aerobic paces from current threshold/CSS, not goal race time.

moderateAerobic.reliabilityNote: Coaches usually set aerobic paces from current threshold/CSS, not goal race time.

threshold.reliabilityNote: Useful guide; confirm with test sets or your coach.

vo2.reliabilityNote: Useful guide; confirm with test sets or your coach.

table.heading: Training Zones
table.subtitle: Paces are per 100 {{unit}}. Adjust based on feel and conditions.
table.hrDisclaimer: Heart rate zones are estimates. Use your own data to refine. Always listen to your body.
table.callout1: Practice paces for {{repDistance}}-{{unit}} repeats, anchored to your {{anchor}}. Pace model: {{paceModel}}.
table.callout2: If your goal time is aspirational, race pace (RP/SP2) and sprint (SP/SP3) are the most reliable targets. Aerobic zones (REC, A1/EN1, A2/EN2) are usually set from current threshold or CSS — confirm with your coach if unsure.
table.caption: Training zone pace targets, effort guidance, and reliability labels.
table.disclaimer: Zone paces are estimates from your goal time ({{paceModel}} model). Treat each pace as roughly ±1–3 seconds per 100. They are not a substitute for lactate testing, CSS benchmarks, or your coach's set design. HR varies by age, fitness, and stroke.
